title: Today's Tech Brief: OpenBSD 7.9 arrives, a diamond in the rough proud of every sharp edge
summary: OpenBSD 7.9 released, Cisco overhauls vulnerability disclosures, and AI accelerates bug hunting.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. First up, OpenBSD 7.9 has arrived, marking the sixtieth release of this security-focused operating system. It's a diamond in the rough, proud of every sharp edge, as the Register puts it. This new version adds support for more cores, delayed hibernation, and basic Wi-Fi 6 capabilities. It's a solid update for those who value simplicity and security. I've always been intrigued by OpenBSD's approach to keeping things lean and mean, and this release doesn't disappoint.

Cisco is overhauling its vulnerability disclosure process, driven in part by AI-accelerated bug hunting. This shift aims to improve how the company handles and shares information about security vulnerabilities. It's an interesting move, as it highlights the growing role of AI in cybersecurity. I've heard some folks in the industry saying that AI is changing the game when it comes to bug hunting, and Cisco's move seems to back that up.

And while we're on the topic of security, it's worth noting that the tech landscape is evolving rapidly. OpenBSD 7.9 demonstrates the ongoing effort to balance functionality with security, while Cisco's move reflects the increasing influence of AI on bug hunting and vulnerability management. It's a lot to keep track of, but it's what makes the tech world so exciting.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
